title: Remove redundant metadata
statement: Do not repeat status, labels, or explanatory metadata already clear from the surrounding object; show only what changes a decision.
rationale: Repeated metadata lowers information density and makes important differences harder to scan.

prefer: Show metadata that changes interpretation or the next action.
Choose one canonical location for each status.
avoid: Repeating labels or status already conveyed by the owning control or section.

when: A dense surface repeats descriptive or status text.
not_when: Redundancy is required for accessibility, safety, or cross-context orientation.

evidence.summary: Asked run-state text be removed when icons communicate it and required overview context to carry into detail rows.
Rejected duplicate status labeling and unnecessary inline detail navigation.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Activated only for the owner-requested initial bootstrap because repeated independent direct instructions or corrections establish this decision boundary.

positive_examples: Show metadata that changes interpretation or the next action.
Choose one canonical location for each status.
negative_examples: Repeating labels or status already conveyed by the owning control or section.

checks: Does each metadata item change a decision?
Is the same status shown elsewhere?
Can a label disappear without ambiguity?
